title: derris lacei
intro: Derris lanceolata, also known as Derris lacei, is a flowering plant that belongs to the legume family Fabaceae. It is native to tropical regions of Asia and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to contain rotenone, a natural insecticide. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Derris lacei are typically made by boiling the leaves or roots in water, and can be used to create teas, infusions, and topical creams.
